content.text:
Done. Changes:

**`src/input-handler.js`** — replaced `previewSwap` + `clearDragPreviews` + `showValidSwapTargets` with a single `updatePreviewState` that updates all drag-related classes (`preview`, `merge-preview`, `unblock-preview`, `swap-dimmed`) in one DOM pass. When a target is active, every other tile gets dimmed (not just non-swappable ones).

**`styles/game.css`** — removed `filter: grayscale(0.2)` from `.gem.swap-dimmed` (filter compositing was the likely culprit for per-tile stagger) and shortened the `.gem` opacity transition from 300ms to 150ms so the dim reads as atomic.

Behavior now:
- Drag start → only non-swappable tiles dim.
- Drag onto valid target → all other tiles dim, leaving source + target + merge-preview + unblock-preview tiles bright.
- Drag back to source → reverts to the start state.

# Match-2048 Project Memory

## RL Training System (branch: claude/nostalgic-ptolemy)
- Worktree at `/Users/garreltmock/.claude-worktrees/match-2048/nostalgic-ptolemy`
- Python (MLX) PPO agent + Node.js env server via JSON stdin/stdout
- Files: `rl/env-server/{server.js,game-env.js}`, `rl/training/{config.py,env_bridge.py,state_encoder.py,network.py,ppo.py,train.py,evaluate.py}`
- ResNet CNN: 2M params, 6 res blocks, 128 channels, NHWC format (MLX)
- State: (8,8,19) tensor, 112 discrete actions (adjacent swaps)
- ~400-500 steps/sec training throughput

## Key Learnings
- MLX Conv2d uses NHWC (channels last), unlike PyTorch NCHW
- Entropy collapses easily with action masking — use high entropy coeff (0.1+)
- Level 13 (blocked rows) is 0% random win — too hard to start RL
- "v6 x1, 30 moves" is good training level (~49% random baseline)
- Greedy eval much worse than stochastic training — expected behavior
- `nn.value_and_grad(self.network, fn)` pattern for MLX PPO

## Simulation Notes
- `simulateMove()` in simulation.js handles `allowNonMatchingSwaps`
- Non-matching swap: valid=true, scoreDelta=0, board persists swapped
- `findAllValidSwaps()` is PRIVATE in hint-system.js (line 68) — not exported (per 2026-04-21 review)
- `createBoard()` in board.js generates random board without initial matches
- Game is stochastic: random tile spawning via `getRandomTileValue()` in board.js:131 (`Math.random()` hardcoded, no RNG injection)

## Feedback
- [Truthful UX in stochastic systems](feedback_truthful_stochastic_ux.md) — deterministic UX claims must be replayable with the same seed in live gameplay
